race.name: Vantaa-hiihto
race.tagline: Thirty to fifty kilometers of mass skiing in the Helsinki metropolitan area — Finland's capital-region citizen race that brings marathon-distance Nordic to the urban doorstep.

climate.description: March in the Helsinki metropolitan area means variable conditions. Temperatures range from -10°C to 3°C. Southern Finland's coastal location makes snow cover less reliable than inland or northern events. Spring sun in early March can soften trails, particularly on south-facing sections.
challenges: Variable snow conditions — southern Finland is snow-marginal
Coastal influence can bring mild spells and rain
Spring sun in March can degrade trail quality

course.primary: A mass skiing course through Vantaa's urban forest and park trail network, offering 30-50km distances. Flat to gently rolling terrain typical of southern Finland's coastal landscape. The course makes long-distance Nordic skiing accessible to Helsinki's large metropolitan skiing population.
course.surface: Machine-groomed urban forest and park trails
features: Helsinki metropolitan area accessibility — large population base
Urban forest and park trail network
Both classic and freestyle techniques offered
Flat terrain suitable for all abilities
Gateway to long-distance skiing for capital-region residents

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum accessibility score — Helsinki metro area provides the largest skiing population base in Finland. Strong community and organization reflect Finland's massahiihto culture and municipal event support. Flat terrain and urban setting limit technicality and scenery. Snow reliability is the key risk in southern coastal Finland.

history.summary: The Vantaa-hiihto is a popular mass skiing event serving Finland's Helsinki metropolitan area, making long-distance Nordic skiing accessible to the capital region's large population. Part of Finland's rich massahiihto (mass skiing) culture, it provides residents of the greater Helsinki area with a marathon-distance ski experience without requiring travel to more remote venues. The event embodies the Finnish tradition that everyone should have access to long-distance skiing.
notable_facts: Serves Finland's largest metropolitan population — Helsinki/Vantaa area
Part of Finnish massahiihto (mass skiing) tradition
Both classic and freestyle techniques offered
One of the most accessible long-distance ski events in Finland
Urban forest setting unique among Nordic ski marathons

videos.title: Yoko Race Day Service, Finnish Championship Team Sprint, Vantaa 12.1.2018
videos.description: Yoko Skis are made in Finland and can and are used by a wide range of racing skiers. A race day ski service is constant in work search of the best possible slide + grip combo, with the test skiers trying variety of different waxing options and the waxing guys adjusting the skis throughout the day. Just how much work, see for yourself how our Racing Service and skiers did in the Finnish Championship Team Sprint Race on Jan 12th, 2018.

videos.title: Euroloppet race BIELA STOPA Slovakia Cross-Country Skiing
videos.description:
Invitation for amazing XC Skiing Marathon race in the central Europe near Kremnica city - Slovakia.
Join EUROPEAN CHAMPIONSHIPS 4 -5.3.2023!

videos.title: Teemu Virtanen’s 24-hour Guinness World Record attempt 2022
videos.description: Live broadcast from Stockholms Stadion. Teemu Virtanen will try to beat the Guiness World Record in cross country skiing in 24 hours. He will start Saturday at 10.00 local time in Stockholm and ski for 24 hours. The goal is to ski more than 470 kilometers.

videos.title: Finlandia-Hiihto 2022 [4K]
videos.description: Vuoden 2022 Finlandia-Hiihdon tunnelmia ja maisemia runsas lumisesta Lahdesta. Lähtö Lahden hiihto stadionilta. Matkan varrelta kuvaa Messilästä ja Tiirismaan vanhan laskettelu-keskuksen kohdilta. Ilmakuvat kuvattu Dji Mavic mini 2 kopterilla.
